title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
summary: Aluminum prices have gained 13% in 2025, with LME futures trading at three-year highs due to China's prevention of overcapacity and supply concerns from boxside grades. The video highlights a projected market deficit of 1.8 million tons in Europe and strong demand driving higher prices forward.

title: Aluminum prices are surging. Here's how companies are handling ...
summary: Aluminum prices on the London Metal Exchange have surged over 13% since U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran in February, reaching 19% higher in 2026 alone. This escalation is causing financial strain on manufacturers across automotive and beverage sectors, with prices hitting peaks unseen since 2022.

title: Aluminum Futures,Aug-2026 (ALI=F)
summary: Aluminum futures for August 2026 rose 1.34% to $3,431.00, with a day's range of $3,374.50 to $3,431.00. The current price reflects strong market momentum amid ongoing supply constraints and rising demand.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: Aluminum futures are trading at $3,410.15, up from a previous close of $3,393.85, with the spot price at $3,408.50. The market shows continued upward pressure as China's Aluminum Corp fell 8.8% to HK$9.42 on Monday.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process designed to recover aluminum ions from aluminum manufacturing waste streams. In lab-scale tests, the membrane captured more than 99% of aluminum ions while allowing other cations to pass through, which could both reduce hazardous waste and improve production yield.

title: U.S. industry exceeds steel capacity utilization level outlined in Section 232 report
summary: This article reports that U.S. steel capacity utilization has risen from 76% last April to above 80% last week, citing American Iron and Steel Institute data. It frames the increase as exceeding the utilization level referenced in the Section 232 report.

title: Steel production up 44.4% year over year after pandemic plunge
summary: The video description says Great Lakes steel production rose by 5,000 tons last week and that U.S. steel mills remained close to 80% of capacity. It presents the gains as part of a broader rebound from the pandemic-era decline.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI reports that in the week ending June 13, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,854,000 net tons and the capability utilization rate was 80.3%. This is the most directly relevant source in the results for current U.S. steel production and capacity figures.

title: Copper futures hover near record highs as risk appetite improves
summary: Copper futures held around $6.5 per pound on Wednesday, near record highs, as market sentiment improved after reports that the US and Iran reached an understanding. The article frames copper’s strength within broader risk-on trading and ongoing tightness in the market.

title: Copper up 9% since Iran war, near January peak
summary: The article says copper prices have risen sharply, supported by geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and strong AI-related demand. It also describes copper as a strategic commodity benefiting from expectations of a prolonged supply crunch.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: EY says copper companies are responding to rising demand by expanding production, investing in sustainability, and adopting digital innovation. The article highlights circular economy practices, responsible mining, and strategic exploration as key themes shaping the sector.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: Tech Xplore reports that copper demand for clean energy could nearly double by 2050, driven by electrification and the energy transition. The article frames copper as a critical material for building a lower-carbon energy system.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: S&P Global projects copper demand rising sharply through 2040, with AI data centers, renewable energy, and electric vehicles driving consumption. The report also notes recycling growth and the importance of new mining supply to meet future needs.

title: Protecting Existing US and Allied Copper Smelting Capacity
source: Columbia University Center on Global Energy Policy
summary: This policy piece discusses the pressures facing copper smelting capacity and the need for modernization tied to environmental compliance. It argues that policy support can help preserve strategic smelting infrastructure while improving sustainability.

title: Circular Economy
summary: The International Copper Association describes copper as a core enabler of the green transition through renewable energy, energy efficiency, and sustainable buildings. The page focuses on circular economy principles as part of copper sustainability efforts.

title: Nickel prices edged lower, then rose to around $17,941 per ton as market sentiment shifted
summary: The article reports that global nickel prices rose 1.01% to $17,941.61 per ton on June 16, 2026, after edging slightly lower the previous day. It links the move to broader market conditions, including developments in global supply and geopolitical news affecting commodities.

title: Nickel traded around $17,900 per tonne as Indonesia mining-policy concerns supported prices
summary: Trading Economics says nickel was trading around $17,900 per tonne, recovering from a two-month low near $17,700. The report says concerns over Indonesia’s mining policies were helping support the market.

title: Nickel prices ready to rise as supply tightens in the fourth quarter
summary: This video discusses nickel market trends and argues that tightening supply could push prices higher in the fourth quarter. It also references Indonesia’s role in the market and notes that prices had been range-bound around $15,000 to $15,800 per ton since April 2025.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been notified it will receive a sharply lower ore production quota for this year. The cut is part of Indonesia's effort to support global nickel prices, which are used in stainless steel and electric-vehicle batteries.

title: Nickel oversupply to persist on expansion, slower demand growth, industry experts say
summary: Reuters reports that global nickel oversupply is expected to continue for several years as production capacity expands faster than demand growth. Industry speakers said new projects in Indonesia are boosting capacity and could keep the surplus in place until 2027-2028.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue. The article says the policy shift also aims to regulate supply volatility and crack down on environmentally harmful operations.

title: Italian steelmaker Cogne Acciai Speciali sourcing all energy from renewables
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali now sources all of its power needs in Europe from renewable energy, with plans to build a hydroelectric plant and electrolyzer at its main Italian site in Aosta. This move underscores the company's commitment to sustainable steel production and reducing its carbon footprint.
